name: Margaret Bernal
outlet: Arizona Department of Health Services - Bureau of Child Care Licensing
story_relevance_note: Bernal is the named Bureau Chief overseeing AZDHS child care facility licensing, the agency at the center of the Maricopa daycare violations data set. Notice-of-inspection-rights documents list her as the public point of contact for licensing complaints.
personalization_hook: Saw your inclusion as Bureau Chief in the CEP's "ADHS Licensing Recommendations" report urging alignment with national CFOC child care standards.

name: AZDHS Public Information
story_relevance_note: Official AZDHS PIO inbox listed on the Director's Office page. Standard channel for media inquiries on licensing actions including the Bureau of Child Care Licensing.

name: DES Child Care Administration
story_relevance_note: DES administers Arizona's child care subsidy. Violations at licensed centers directly impact families using DES vouchers. Listed as the official program contact on the DES child care services page.
personalization_hook: Saw your work clearing the child care subsidy waitlist with $45 million in funding for families earning up to 100% of the Federal Poverty Level since August 2024.

name: DCS Policy Unit
story_relevance_note: DCS is the cross-referenced reporting body when daycare violations include suspected abuse or neglect. The Policy Unit fields formal inquiries from advocates and journalists about systemic issues spanning licensing and child welfare.
personalization_hook: Saw your updates to the **DCS Program Policy** manual on safety and risk assessments in child protection cases.

name: Melinda Gulick
beat: early childhood policy and quality care
story_relevance_note: Gulick leads Arizona's statewide early childhood agency, which funds Quality First and partners with AZDHS on provider quality. The central community inbox routes to executive leadership and is the listed contact for media and policy inquiries on early childhood quality.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Coming together to support Arizona's youngest children" on First Things First's site.

name: Angela Rabago
beat: government affairs and tribal affairs for early childhood
story_relevance_note: As FTF's Chief External Affairs Officer, Rabago leads government and policy engagement on early childhood quality. She is the relevant counterpart for Arizona child care licensing reform conversations and a likely amplifier of investigative findings.

name: Casey Cullings
story_relevance_note: Cullings has served as FTF's first in-house counsel since 2014 and is the lead for legal and regulatory questions on Arizona early childhood programs. Useful contact for legal context on AZDHS licensing authority and provider compliance.

name: Christina Lyons
story_relevance_note: Lyons is the FTF senior director closest to regional councils, including the Northwest Maricopa region where daycare violations cluster. She is the bridge between statewide policy and local provider engagement.

name: Wendy Sabatini
story_relevance_note: Sabatini directly leads FTF's Maricopa Region. Maricopa is the primary geography for the daycare-violations data set, making her the single most relevant FTF regional voice on the story.

name: Julia Chavez
story_relevance_note: Chavez is the executive office gatekeeper for FTF Board engagement, which includes the AZDHS Director as a sitting member. Useful path to land the data set in front of the joint state agency leadership reviewing child care quality.

name: Ofelia Gonzalez
outlet: Quality First (administered by First Things First)
beat: Quality First media inquiries, Arizona child care quality rating
story_relevance_note: Gonzalez is the named media contact for Quality First, Arizona's child care quality rating and improvement system. Quality First scores intersect directly with AZDHS licensing actions, making her a critical voice on whether quality ratings flagged the violating facilities.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Newly awarded grant opens more access to Quality First" on expanding Rating Only participation in February 2026.

name: Quality First Information Desk
outlet: Quality First (administered by First Things First)
story_relevance_note: Public Quality First inbox listed on the program's contact page. Routes parent and policy inquiries about provider quality scores and is the program's documented channel for non-PIO inquiries.

name: Candida Hunter-Querta
beat: tribal early childhood programs in Arizona
story_relevance_note: Hunter-Querta is FTF's Senior Director of Tribal Affairs, the leadership voice on early childhood quality across Arizona's 22 tribal nations. Tribal child care providers operate under different licensing regimes and are an under-covered angle in any AZDHS daycare violations investigation.

name: Luisa Santos Martinez
beat: media inquiries and child policy communications
story_relevance_note: Santos Martinez is the named media contact for Children's Action Alliance per the Media Inquiries page. Direct line for landing investigative findings with CAA's policy and comms team.

name: Michelle Crow
beat: Southern Arizona child policy and coalitions
story_relevance_note: Crow leads CAA's Southern Arizona work and convenes regional coalitions. Useful for statewide framing beyond Maricopa and for building advocacy pressure outside Phoenix.

name: Eve DelReal
role: Director of Head Start (and President, Arizona Head Start Association)
beat: Head Start program operations and statewide early childhood
story_relevance_note: DelReal directs Head Start at SWHD and serves as President of the Arizona Head Start Association. Head Start providers operate under federal performance standards parallel to AZDHS state licensing, and DelReal is the senior voice on systemic quality issues across Arizona Head Start centers, many in Maricopa.
personalization_hook: Saw your discussion on “Head Start federal program faces citizenship rule pause” on Arizona PBS Horizon in August 2025.

name: Jenni Adams
beat: early childhood, Birth to Five Helpline, family support
story_relevance_note: Adams is the published news media contact at Southwest Human Development, Arizona's largest early childhood nonprofit serving 140,000 children and families. SWHD operates the Birth to Five Helpline, which fields parent calls about child care concerns.
personalization_hook: Saw your interview on Horizon PBS Arizona discussing the Birth to Five Helpline.

name: SWHD Center of Excellence
outlet: Southwest Human Development - Birth to Five Center of Excellence
beat: infant and early childhood mental health, training and consultation
story_relevance_note: Center of Excellence trains Arizona child care professionals on infant and early childhood mental health, including trauma response. Direct stakeholder when licensing violations involve harm to children.

name: AZ Department of Education Early Childhood
beat: K-3 standards intersecting with preschool and child care quality
story_relevance_note: ADE's Early Childhood Education unit is the public schooling-side counterpart to FTF and AZDHS on early childhood quality. Listed on the official ADE agency directory.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Arizona Department of Education Early Childhood Unit Inclusion" training proposal on developing statewide capacity for the Inclusion Capacity Profile (ICP).

name: ADE Foster Care Education
story_relevance_note: Foster-care-involved children are statistically more likely to be in subsidized child care settings. ADE's foster care unit fields cross-system inquiries on child safety.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Foster Care Education: Basics" one-pager dated August 15, 2024, on school stability for foster youth.

name: AZDHS Press Office
outlet: Arizona Department of Health Services - Director's Office
beat: AZDHS director's office press inquiries, including licensing actions
story_relevance_note: pio@azdhs.gov is the active press contact listed on AZDHS director's office news releases under Director Jennie Cunico. This is the canonical channel for media inquiries on Bureau of Child Care Licensing actions.
personalization_hook: Saw your November 27, 2024 news release on the Avanti Senior Living licensing update under new ownership as Spring Gardens.

name: Irene McKisson
story_relevance_note: AZ Luminaria runs deep investigative work in Pima/border. Route any major investigation here (charter, opioid Walgreens 350M, daycare violations, ICE detention).
personalization_hook: Saw your coverage of Pima County Sheriff's Department violating its own policy tracking calls to Border Patrol.

name: Yana Kunichoff
role: Reporter, Report For America corps member
story_relevance_note: RFA corps, criminal justice + education. Strong match for AZ educator discipline, school restraint data (use of force on students), career schools adverse actions, daycare violations (childcare oversight).
personalization_hook: Saw your March 24, 2026 piece "In Cochise County, a mobile team brings help and connection" in AZ Luminaria on community health outreach.

name: Beatriz Limon
story_relevance_note: Bilingual reporter/translator. Use for stories with Spanish-speaking impact populations: ICE detention, daycare violations in Latino communities, opioid harm in border Walgreens corridor.

name: Jennifer Goldberg
role: Senior Editor, Culture; Best of Phoenix Editor
story_relevance_note: Culture. Lower priority for hard-news investigations but useful when stories have local Phoenix-life cultural angle (e.g., daycare violations in specific Phoenix neighborhoods).
personalization_hook: Saw you oversee the "Best of Phoenix®" roundup that hit newsstands around the Valley.

name: Tirion Boan
story_relevance_note: Food beat. Skip routing for hard investigations unless food-safety angle (e.g., daycare meal safety).
personalization_hook: Saw your coverage of restaurant openings and closings as Food Editor since May 2022.

name: Sara Crocker
story_relevance_note: GA writer. Match for any active Phoenix-metro investigation: Mesa HB2228 elder abuse, ASU Prep, daycare violations, AZ educator discipline, Walgreens opioid.

name: Pam Ashley
story_relevance_note: Features. Strong match for grand court Mesa elder abuse (human-interest victim angle), daycare violations, school restraint stories with named-family angle.
